title: Payhip Alternatives for African Digital Creators: Which Option Works Regardless of Where You Are — The Honest Version
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: Having a great digital product and being unable to collect payment is a frustrating experience many African creators face, with platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip often being unavailable due to geographic restrictions, this leaves many wondering how they can sell their digital products and get paid without these conventional platforms, the search for alternatives is not just about finding a similar service, but about finding one that actually works regardless of where you are, for many creators in Nigeria and other restricted countries, this search has been a long and disappointing one, but there are solutions that can help you sell your digital products and get paid without the need for these conventional platforms
What You Are Actually Comparing

When looking for Payhip alternatives, it's essential to understand what you are actually comparing, the traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip, have a complex system of compliance decisions, fraud risk tiers, and banking infrastructure, that often exclude creators in certain countries, this means that even if you find an alternative, it may still have the same restrictions, so it's crucial to look for platforms that have a different approach, one that is more inclusive and flexible, and can provide a seamless payment experience for both you and your customers
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

The traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip, break down when it comes to serving creators in restricted countries, they often require a payment processor account, which can be difficult to obtain, and even if you manage to get one, the fees and charges can be high, and the payment process can be slow and cumbersome, this is because these platforms are designed with a specific geographic compliance layer, that prioritizes certain countries over others, leaving many creators without a reliable way to collect payment
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, does things differently, it has no geographic compliance layer, which means that it can serve creators regardless of where they are, it also has a more flexible and inclusive approach, that allows for instant automated delivery, and global reach, without the need for a payment processor account, or high fees and charges, this is because crypto-native infrastructure is built on blockchain technology, which is decentralized and borderless, making it possible to create a more level playing field for creators
PayPal not being available in your country is not a you problem — it is a platform design decision that you are not obligated to accept.
The Real Cost Difference

When it comes to the cost, the traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip, often have a subscription-based model, which can be expensive, especially for creators who are just starting out, on the other hand, crypto-native infrastructure, like Unchained Commerce, offers a one-time payment option, with lifetime access, and no subscriptions, this means that you can get started with a low upfront cost, and without the burden of recurring fees, which can be a significant advantage for creators who are looking to keep their costs low
Which One to Use and Why

So, which one to use and why, the answer is simple, if you are a creator in a restricted country, and you want a reliable way to collect payment, without the hassle and expense of traditional platforms, then crypto-native infrastructure is the way to go, it offers a more flexible and inclusive approach, with instant automated delivery, and global reach, without the need for a payment processor account, or high fees and charges, and with a one-time payment option, and lifetime access, it's a no-brainer

For creators in Nigeria and other restricted countries, who want to sell digital products without PayPal, the solution is to use a crypto-native infrastructure, like Unchained Commerce, which offers a range of products, including the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, these products provide a seamless payment experience, with instant automated delivery, and global reach, without the need for a payment processor account, or high fees and charges, and with a one-time payment option, and lifetime access, you can get started with a low upfront cost, and without the burden of recurring fees, which can be a significant advantage for creators who are looking to keep their costs low
A platform that can decide your country does not qualify is a platform you are renting from on their terms, not infrastructure you own.
Related Topics Worth Reading

For creators who are looking for a Gumroad alternative, or a way to sell online without a Stripe account, the solution is the same, crypto-native infrastructure, offers a more flexible and inclusive approach, that can provide a reliable way to collect payment, without the hassle and expense of traditional platforms, and with the range of products available, you can find the one that best suits your needs, whether you are selling ebooks, courses, tutorials, art, music, software, fonts, presets, templates, or digital products

Before you launch your digital product store, make sure you have the right infrastructure in place, start by setting up your Core Shop Engine, which will provide the backend with automated delivery, and payment verification, then integrate your Web3 Wallet Connect, which will give you frontend wallet integration, and multi-chain QR codes, and live price feeds, and finally, set up your Admin Dashboard, which will provide you with transaction logs, order management, and real-time analytics, with these products, you can create a seamless payment experience, with instant automated delivery, and global reach, without the need for a payment processor account, or high fees and charges
